_comment: Example custom workflow shape. Built-in workflows (feature, bug-fix, refactor, idea-exploration, greenfield) live in forgewright/workflows/*.json and are used as-is when not overridden here. Define a workflow under a NEW name to add it; redefining a built-in name (e.g. "feature") overrides the built-in entirely — be intentional about that. Every phase requires a `name` field — non-empty, identifier-safe ([A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]*), unique within the workflow. Names appear in logs, status output, peer task_ref strings, and idempotence prompts; pick something readable.

solo-feature._comment: Single-agent variant of the feature workflow — no peer handoffs. Useful when you want forgewright's plan→audit→verify→test loop without dispatching implementation to peers. Per-workflow `reaudit` block overrides the top-level reaudit config — every key is overridable (maxCycles, minDeltaPercent, minDeltaLines, decisionMode, loopableStages); missing keys fall through to the top-level defaults. The merged config is frozen onto the workflow at start time so later edits don't retroactively change a running workflow.

phases.name: plan-review
phases.summary: Review the plan and audit findings, then implement the plan steps locally. Resume when done.

quick-feature._comment: Lean feature workflow with peer handoffs — plan, dispatch implementation to peers, run tests. Skips plan-quality-review, the audit pipeline, and the test-coverage handoff. Use when you want forgewright's planning + peer dispatch but not the full audit/verification loop. Per-workflow `tests.command` overrides the top-level `tests.command` for ${TEST_CMD} substitution — handy when this workflow needs a different test command than the project default (e.g., a fast smoke suite instead of the full integration run).

phases.name: plan-review
phases.summary: Review the plan. Resume to dispatch implementation to peers.

phases.name: implement
phases.directive: Implement the plan in artifacts/plan.md. Decompose into independent tasks (per file scope or per plan step) and dispatch to available peers. Run any tasks yourself if no peers are available.

model-eval._comment: Example of a non-test `command` phase using the optional `instruction` field together with multi-output `produces`. The script writes several files (metrics + model + log) directly into the artifacts dir using the filenames declared in `produces` — each entry registers under its stem so downstream phases can `consumes` any of them. ${ARTIFACTS} in the command string substitutes to the workflow's artifacts dir. The decision goes into mcpResult `summary` (free-form string — JSON blob is fine).

phases.name: train-and-eval
phases.instruction: Read the metrics artifact and compare new_model.sharpe to baseline.sharpe. If new_model wins by >=0.05, advance --result completed with summary='{"decision":"accept","delta":<n>}'. If it loses or is flat, advance --result completed with summary='{"decision":"reject","delta":<n>}'. If the metrics file is missing or malformed, advance --result failed.

train-then-backtest._comment: Two command phases chained via consumes + ${ARTIFACT.<stem>} interpolation. Phase 1 writes model.bin to the artifacts dir; phase 2 reads it via ${ARTIFACT.model}. `consumes` declares the dependency so a missing/renamed upstream artifact fails at descriptor-build time (loudly) rather than mid-command. Add as many ${ARTIFACT.<stem>} tokens as you like — each is resolved against the registered path.

phases.name: backtest
phases.instruction: Read the backtest artifact and report summary='{"sharpe":<n>,"max_dd":<n>}'. If backtest.json is missing or malformed, advance --result failed.
